explicit_text: I was born in Mexico City, Mexico, and my roots are deeply Mexican. I have lived in the U.S. for over two decades, but Mexico City remains my cultural and familial home. I send money back to my family there because the economic situation is difficult.

explicit_text: Mexico is my home country where my parents and sisters live. Even though I have lived in the U.S. for 22 years, I still support my family there financially. The economic hardship in Mexico affects my ability to help them, especially during the pandemic.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic affected me deeply; I was suspended from work for a whole year and had to find other jobs to survive. I faced many challenges, including irregular hours, low pay, and health risks at work. I also had two car accidents during this time, which made working even harder.

explicit_text: I was not able to receive any help from the CARES Act because I don't have legal immigration status or a social security number. This lack of access to federal aid made it very difficult to cover rent and other expenses during the pandemic. Many others in my situation also missed out on this support.
